acceptOwnership().details: The new owner accepts the ownership transfer.

acceptPendingMinApy().details: Can only be called after the timelock period has elapsed.      Finalizes the APY change and updates the active minimum APY.

params.deltaFt: The change in the ft balance of the order

approve(address,uint256).details: See {IERC20-approve}. NOTE: If `value` is the maximum `uint256`, the allowance is not updated on `transferFrom`. This is semantically equivalent to an infinite approval. Requirements: - `spender` cannot be the zero address.

apy().details: APY is calculated based on the vault's current performance and accruing interest.      This is a dynamic value that reflects real-time vault performance.

returns._0: The current APY as a uint256 value (e.g., 5% APY = 0.05e8)

createOrder(address,(uint256,uint256,uin.details: Deploys a new TermMax order contract with the given configuration.      The order will be associated with the specified market and pool.

params.market: The TermMax market address that the order will operate in
params.params: The configuration parameters for the new order

returns.order: The address of the newly created TermMax order contract

decimals().details: Decimals are computed by adding the decimal offset on top of the underlying asset's decimals. This "original" value is cached during construction of the vault contract. If this read operation fails (e.g., the asset has not been created yet), a default of 18 is used to represent the underlying asset's decimals. See {IERC20Metadata-decimals}.

initialize((address,address,address,uint.details: This function should only be called once during contract deployment.      Sets up initial vault configuration including APY parameters, access controls,      and initial pool configurations.

params.params: The initial configuration parameters for the vault including:               - minApy: minimum guaranteed APY               - minIdleFundRate: minimum rate for idle funds               - governance and admin addresses               - initial pool configurations

minApy().details: This represents the floor APY that the vault aims to maintain.      Changes to this value require timelock approval for security.

returns._0: The minimum APY as a uint64 value (e.g., 5% APY = 0.05e8)

name().details: Returns the name of the token.

owner().details: Returns the address of the current owner.

paused().details: Returns true if the contract is paused, and false otherwise.

pendingMinApy().details: Contains the proposed new value and timing information for the pending change.      Used to track timelock status and proposed changes.

returns._0: PendingUint192 struct with pending minimum APY data, structure includes:         - newValue: the proposed new minimum APY         - validAt: the timestamp when the change becomes valid         - isActive: whether there's an active pending change

pendingOwner().details: Returns the address of the pending owner.

pendingPool().details: Contains the proposed new value and timing information for the pending change.      Used to track timelock status and proposed changes.

returns._0: PendingAddress struct with pending pool data, structure includes:         - newValue: the proposed new pool address         - validAt: the timestamp when the change becomes valid

params.orders: The list of order addresses to update
params.removedLiquidities: The amount of liquidity to remove from each order

renounceOwnership().details: Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner.

revokePendingPool().details: Cancels the pending change and resets the pending state.      Allows governance to abort pool changes before they take effect.

submitPendingMinApy(uint64).details: Initiates a timelock period before the new minimum APY can be applied.      Only authorized governance can call this function.

params.newMinApy: The proposed new minimum APY value (e.g., 5% APY = 0.05e8)

symbol().details: Returns the symbol of the token, usually a shorter version of the name.

totalAssets().details: Get total assets, falling back to real assets if virtual assets exceed limit

transfer(address,uint256).details: See {IERC20-transfer}. Requirements: - `to` cannot be the zero address. - the caller must have a balance of at least `value`.

transferFrom(address,address,uint256).details: See {IERC20-transferFrom}. Skips emitting an {Approval} event indicating an allowance update. This is not required by the ERC. See {xref-ERC20-_approve-address-address-uint256-bool-}[_approve]. NOTE: Does not update the allowance if the current allowance is the maximum `uint256`. Requirements: - `from` and `to` cannot be the zero address. - `from` must have a balance of at least `value`. - the caller must have allowance for ``from``'s tokens of at least `value`.

transferOwnership(address).details: Starts the ownership transfer of the contract to a new account. Replaces the pending transfer if there is one. Can only be called by the current owner. Setting `newOwner` to the zero address is allowed; this can be used to cancel an initiated ownership transfer.

params.orderConfigs: The new configuration parameters for each order, containing:                    - virtualXtReserve: The new virtual XT reserve for the order                    - maxXtReserve: The new maximum XT reserve for the order                    - removingLiquidity: The amount of liquidity to remove from the order                    - curveCuts: The new curve cuts for the order
params.orders: The list of order addresses to update

acceptPendingMinApy().notice: Accepts and applies the pending minimum APY change

apy().notice: Returns the current annual percentage yield based on accreting principal

createOrder(address,(uint256,uint256,uin.notice: Creates a new order with the specified parameters

initialize((address,address,address,uint.notice: Initializes the vault with the provided parameters

minApy().notice: Returns the minimum guaranteed APY for the vault

pendingMinApy().notice: Returns the pending minimum APY update details

removeLiquidityFromOrders(address[],uint.notice: Remove the liquidity from multiple orders

submitPendingMinApy(uint64).notice: Submits a new minimum APY for pending approval

updateOrdersConfiguration(address[],(uin.notice: Update the configuration for multiple orders
